video_title: SpaceX IPO Mania and Ethena’s Strategic Pivot
gist: Blockworks analysts argue the record SpaceX IPO will siphon speculative liquidity from Bitcoin (already in 5+ weeks of ETF outflows near 63K), but on-chain indicators like realized price (~53K) and MVRV-Z suggest a cycle low forms by Q4. They also dissect Ethena's pivot from a basis-trade wrapper to a diversified on-chain yield curator and debate whether to turn on its fee switch.

themes.theme: SpaceX IPO and pre-IPO market dynamics

insights.claim: SpaceX's IPO is the largest ever, priced at $135/share, while Hyperliquid's pre-IPO market is pricing it around $177/share, implying market participants expect a premium to the IPO price.
insights.speaker: Luke Leisure
insights.so_what: Watch for convergence: when the registered security goes live, the trading price tends to converge to the higher Hyperliquid pre-IPO price, a repeatable pattern seen with prior listings.
insights.quote: it's being priced at around 177 a share where the IPO price came in at 135

insights.claim: On Hyperliquid the SpaceX pre-IPO market saw $373M of 24-hour volume and nearly $300M of open interest, up ~4x in a day, already ranking as a top-10 HIP-3 market with potential for up to $3B in 24-hour volume.
insights.speaker: Luke Leisure
insights.so_what: Validates Hyperliquid's pre-IPO/HIP-3 product as a real venue capturing IPO speculation flow, a bullish signal for Hyperliquid's fee capture and market-share narrative.
insights.quote: volumes at 373 mil over the past 24 hours and uh just short of 300 mil in open interest and that's up about 4x from this time yesterday

insights.claim: Pre-IPO markets like Hyperliquid's converge to actual opening prices after IPO, as observed with Cerebras, where the market priced a premium and the stock popped further once the opening trade hit the tape.
insights.speaker: Danny
insights.so_what: Pre-IPO market premiums can be used as a leading indicator for IPO-day pops, a tradeable signal for new high-profile listings.

insights.claim: SpaceX could become one of the most valuable companies in the world within a lifetime, but the stock could also be 80% off its IPO price within roughly two years.
insights.speaker: Luke Leisure
insights.so_what: A barbell view warning against bidding the IPO on day one; post-IPO unlock-driven drawdowns make early entry risky despite a strong long-term thesis.
insights.quote: I also wouldn't be surprised to see it 80% off its IPO price within two years or so

insights.claim: The SpaceX IPO's high retail allocation, well above historical IPO precedent, combined with its low-float high-valuation structure makes its chart likely to resemble a low-float high-FDV token launch with many sellers waiting at unlocks.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Treat the SpaceX listing with low-float/high-FDV mental models: narrative-driven pop followed by supply overhang and lockup expiry selling.
insights.quote: this reminds me of like any of those lowflat high FDV launches

insights.claim: The SpaceX IPO is analogous to the Trump coin launch for equity markets, likely to suck attention and liquidity out of other speculative assets.
insights.speaker: Carlos
insights.so_what: Expect a temporary liquidity drain on crypto and risk assets around the IPO event, a setup for a sentiment/liquidity low.
insights.quote: the SpaceX IPO is kind of like the Trump coin launch for meme coins uh for equity markets

insights.claim: Anthropic and OpenAI have reportedly already filed confidential IPO paperwork, raising the prospect of a wave of fresh equity liquidity hitting markets simultaneously over the next few months.
insights.speaker: Danny
insights.so_what: A cluster of mega AI IPOs could compound the liquidity drain on crypto in the near term, a macro headwind to monitor for risk-asset timing.
insights.quote: We've got um confidential filings from Anthropic and OpenAI supposedly already in

themes.theme: Bitcoin weakness, ETF outflows and Sailor risk

insights.claim: Bitcoin ETFs have seen over five consecutive weeks of net outflows totaling more than $5B, with roughly $200M in outflows in the latest daily figure, signaling traditional investors are reducing exposure.
insights.speaker: Carlos
insights.so_what: Sustained ETF outflows mark the marginal seller; a reversal in ETF flow direction would be an early confirmation signal for a Bitcoin bottom.
insights.quote: we have over I think it's five consecutive weeks at this point of consecutive uh outflows for Bitcoin. Uh it's over five billion I believe in outflows

insights.claim: AI and semiconductor equities have ripped versus Bitcoin for the past year, capturing the 'hot ball of money'; eventually that pair gets overbought and the relative-value trade rotates back to crypto.
insights.speaker: Luke Leisure
insights.so_what: A semis/AI-vs-Bitcoin ratio at extremes is a setup for mean-reversion rotation back into crypto, a relative-value trade to watch.

insights.claim: MicroStrategy faces a 'trilemma' among the Bitcoin price, MSTR common equity price, and the STRC/Stretch preferred price, where one instrument must be sacrificed for the other two; strategy appears willing to sacrifice MSTR common in favor of senior/preferred debt holders.
insights.speaker: Luke Leisure
insights.so_what: Implies downside risk concentrated in MSTR common equity rather than its preferred instruments; informs how to position across the MicroStrategy capital stack.
insights.quote: There's the Bitcoin price, the micro strategy common price and the stretch price. And it seems like one of those instruments kind of needs to get sacrificed in favor of the other two

insights.claim: MicroStrategy is not heavily levered and is not the marginal flow by size relative to ETF flows, so it is not the dominant driver of Bitcoin's price compared to the ETFs.
insights.speaker: Luke Leisure
insights.so_what: Tempers fears that a Sailor blowup would crater Bitcoin; ETF flows, not MicroStrategy, are the variable to track for spot direction.

insights.claim: Sailor being potentially tapped out as a Bitcoin buyer, plus Bitcoin miners transitioning hash rate to AI compute, are two structural catalysts that could help form a Bitcoin bottom.
insights.speaker: Danny
insights.so_what: Miner offramping to AI alters mining economics; once enough hash rate leaves, mining a Bitcoin becomes attractive again, creating an equilibrium-driven floor to watch.

insights.claim: Bitcoin's realized price (the on-chain volume-weighted average cost basis of all coins) sits around $53K and is viewed as fair value; tagging or discounting that level would signal a bear-market low is in.
insights.speaker: Luke Leisure
insights.so_what: With spot near 63.3K, realized price at ~53K implies ~10-15% downside to a high-conviction accumulation zone; a discount to it is the long-term buy signal.
insights.quote: fair value in the long run is to me it's it's the realized price

insights.claim: The Bitcoin MVRV-Z score is approaching the green zone marking historical lows, suggesting a cycle low could be set by Q4 and that it is a good time to become increasingly optimistic on Bitcoin.
insights.speaker: Luke Leisure
insights.so_what: MVRV-Z nearing historically reliable low zones gives a Q4 timing anchor for a Bitcoin bottom and accumulation.
insights.quote: I think by like by Q4 that that lows set

insights.claim: Bitcoin is trading near range lows around 63.3K and could take out that low over the next few months, after which downside risk is largely exhausted and risk shifts back to the upside.
insights.speaker: Luke Leisure
insights.so_what: A final flush below range lows would likely mark capitulation; positions skew favorably to the upside thereafter.
insights.quote: Bitcoin's teetering at range lows right now, 63.3K

insights.claim: Practical accumulation levels are 57-58K and a deeper wick, with Bitcoin's weekly RSI dipping into oversold historically marking strong bottoms; another 5-10% drawdown in SPY would likely push Bitcoin into those buy zones.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Ties Bitcoin's buy zone to equity drawdown; watch weekly RSI oversold plus a 5-10% SPY pullback as a combined accumulation trigger.

insights.claim: On-chain bottom indicators historically flash buy precisely during existential crisis events (e.g., 2022 with 3AC and FTX), so reaching the green zone coincides with extreme fear, not an easy mechanical buy.
insights.speaker: Danny
insights.so_what: Cautions against treating cycle-low indicators as risk-free; the buy zone arrives with genuine narrative-shifting fear, demanding conviction to act.
insights.quote: the future's always changing. So, I think it's just something to keep in mind that it's not it's not as simple as just, you know, the lines have already been drawn

insights.claim: When Bitcoin dipped below its 200-week moving average in 2022 it traded there for about six months, showing on-chain buy signals can be correct yet require a long holding period rather than a V-shaped recovery.
insights.speaker: Carlos
insights.so_what: Sets time-horizon expectations: even valid buy zones can persist for months, so size and patience matter more than precise bottom-timing.
insights.quote: in 2022 when when it dipped below the 200 week moving average people were calling the bottom but it traded there for like six months

insights.claim: Because Bitcoin is a store-of-value asset valued subjectively rather than on forward earnings, technical and on-chain indicators matter more for it than for traditional equities since they drive participants' buy/sell decisions.
insights.speaker: Carlos
insights.so_what: Justifies a technicals-first framework for Bitcoin timing versus a fundamentals-first one for equities.

themes.theme: Ethena's pivot from basis trade to yield curator

insights.claim: The crypto basis (cash-and-carry) trade has collapsed during this bear market, making it unattractive and pushing Ethena to reallocate toward stablecoin lending and other instruments.
insights.speaker: Luke Leisure
insights.so_what: Ethena's yield source is structurally shifting; sUSDe yield will increasingly depend on lending and RWA strategies rather than funding rates.
insights.quote: during this bear that the basis on crypto has just collapsed and that cash and carry trade really isn't too attractive

insights.claim: Basis-trade yields have compressed across successive bull periods (around 20% post-Trump election, then ~15%, then ~8-10% in the latest cycle) because the trade is being commoditized as more institutions and professionals run it.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Structural yield compression means even bullish periods won't restore old basis returns, validating Ethena's diversification and lowering forward sUSDe yield expectations.
insights.quote: it was 20 then 15 and then last one during the debt mania it was around 8 to 10%

insights.claim: Ethena realistically has only three scalable capital-deployment options: institutional lending, AAA CLOs, and existing DeFi lending strategies (boosted by incentives on venues like Mega ETH or Plasma), while gold and equity basis trades are too small to matter.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Defines the actual levers behind future sUSDe yield; gold has less open interest than Solana, so RWA gold/equity trades are non-factors until they scale dramatically.
insights.quote: Etheina has only like three options to deploy capital into one is institutional lending the other one is AAAL's and the last one is their current existing defy lending strategies

insights.claim: Maple Finance's syrupUSDC grew from a few hundred million in AUM in early last year to several billion dollars, with yields falling from 10%+ at launch toward roughly 5% as institutional lending players like Falcon X and Coinbase Asset Management entered.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Shows institutional lending is scalable but yield-compressing as competition rises; sets ~5% as the realistic baseline for Ethena's lending-derived yield.
insights.quote: they started with I think just a few hundred million in AUM in Jan last year and now it's uh a few billion dollars already

insights.claim: Maple's institutional lending model is attractive because collateral can be escrowed at a third-party custodian and loans are fixed-rate/fixed-term, eliminating much of the exploit and interest-rate-variance risk seen in recent on-chain money market and RS exploits.
insights.speaker: Luke Leisure
insights.so_what: Explains why Maple kept growing deposits despite a contracting lending market; custody plus fixed terms are the structural edge institutional capital pays for.

insights.claim: Ethena's diversification was driven more by risk reduction than yield gains, since after the Kelp incident it needed to cut reliance on DeFi lending where roughly half of its stables sit (with much of the remainder in T-bills) and where yields are propped up by fading incentives.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Reframes the reserve change as de-risking concentration rather than yield maximization, important for assessing sUSDe stability through redemption events.
insights.quote: around half the stables are actually in D5 lending and uh a significant portion of the remaining stables are in T bills

insights.claim: Ethena is effectively converging on the model Sky (formerly MakerDAO) pioneered years ago, becoming a vertically integrated on-chain asset manager rather than a pure basis-trade runner, whereas Sky outsources allocation to agents like Obex, Spark and Grove.
insights.speaker: Carlos
insights.so_what: Positions Ethena as an on-chain hedge fund / asset manager; its valuation and competitive set shift toward yield curators like Sky and Spark.
insights.quote: Athena is kind of converging upon what Sky has pursued for the past uh few years

insights.claim: Ethena must hold variable-duration strategies that can be unwound to meet potential multi-billion-dollar redemptions within a 48-hour window during a deleveraging event, balancing redemption liquidity against yield.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Liquidity laddering of reserves is the key solvency constraint; structured fixed-term loans must be matched to redemption risk for sUSDe stability.

insights.claim: The new reserve strategies should produce a modest uptick in sUSDe yield but collapse its volatility, ending the era of 20-30% fixed-yield opportunities on sUSDe principal tokens; Ethena retains optionality to switch back into the basis trade for 7-8% in bullish periods.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Lower, steadier yields make sUSDe less of a high-fixed-yield trade on Pendle but more reliable; the basis-trade optionality is Ethena's edge over fixed competitors like Spark.
insights.quote: I fond memories of the opportunities to clip 20 to 30% fixed on an SUSD principal token

insights.claim: A potential Coinbase partnership could give Ethena a powerful distribution channel, mirroring Coinbase's earlier Morpho integration that lets users lend stablecoins and Bitcoin for yield, helping reverse Ethena's TVL outflows even at a modest 4.5-5% yield.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Coinbase distribution is a key catalyst that could re-grow Ethena TVL and reshape its deposit base toward retail seeking a 1-2% spread over T-bills.
insights.quote: this Coinbase partnership announcement could be the one that kind of changes the game a little and kind of changes the the deposit base of Athena

themes.theme: ENA token value accrual and fee switch debate

insights.claim: Ethena should lower its fee-switch hurdle from the original 6-7% spread over a 3% sUSDe to roughly a 1-2% spread above T-bills, since the old KPIs no longer fit its new yield-curator model.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Re-setting the hurdle rate raises the probability the fee switch turns on, reducing the discount investors must apply to ENA value accrual.
insights.quote: they need like a 6 to 7% spread over SUSDs which is at 3%. Like come on. I mean that's no longer reasonable

insights.claim: Modeling a 4% take on yield, ENA buybacks would range roughly 3-7% of supply at a ~0.40% lean, but with about 1.8% of ENA supply unlocking monthly until 2028 the buybacks would be dwarfed by unlocks.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Quantifies that fee-switch buybacks won't offset emissions; the value of turning it on is signaling token alignment, not absorbing the unlock overhang.
insights.quote: around 1.8% of the silk supply gets unlocked each month. So this buy will be nothing close to the amount of unlocks

insights.claim: Even if buybacks are immaterial, turning on the fee switch is worth it to signal to the market that the ENA token is tied to the protocol and not just an orphaned asset.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Argues token-protocol alignment signaling can re-rate ENA independent of buyback magnitude; a catalyst for sentiment if announced.

insights.claim: Morpho trades at a large premium to other lending protocols despite zero value accrual to its token, because the market prices in its Coinbase distribution, where about 63% of Morpho's stablecoin deposits come from Coinbase users.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Distribution can earn a token premium even without fee capture; if Ethena secures similar Coinbase ties, the market may award it a comparable re-rating.
insights.quote: out of Morphos I think uh 63% of the stable coin deposits actually come from Coinbase users

insights.claim: Growth-mode with no fee capture can be an optimal strategy to win market share, so Ethena should first execute the reserve shift and improve sUSDe APY before recommunicating lowered fee-switch metrics.
insights.speaker: Luke Leisure
insights.so_what: Treat early-stage protocols like growth-stage companies (infinite PE is acceptable); sequencing execution before fee capture is the playbook to watch for re-rating.
insights.quote: growth mode and no fee capture can actually be a really really good strategy to get market share

insights.claim: As tokenized equities and real businesses come on chain, protocols whose tokens lack equity-like rights and value accrual will be increasingly punished, since numerous past acquisitions and deals have been zeros for token holders.
insights.speaker: Danny
insights.so_what: Token-equity alignment (foundation/token unified structure, value accrual) becomes a differentiator; misaligned tokens face structural derating as competition with tokenized equities grows.
insights.quote: as we've seen with with countless acquisitions and other deals over the past one two years now that have just been zeros for token holders

insights.claim: Last year Ethena used the StablecoinX vehicle to provide liquidity/buy back ENA for private investors rather than relying solely on open-market buys, but it did not meaningfully support the token's price over subsequent months.
insights.speaker: Carlos
insights.so_what: Prior buyback-style efforts failed to hold ENA's price, suggesting unlocks dominate until TVL/revenue growth makes the token demonstrably cheap.
